title: Chartered surveyors
description: Research, study, and prepare maps and other spatial data in digital or graphic form for one or more purposes, such as legal, social, political, educational, and design purposes. May work with Geographic Information Systems (GIS). May design and evaluate algorithms, data structures, and user interfaces for GIS and mapping systems. May collect, analyze, and interpret geographic information provided by geodetic surveys, aerial photographs, and satellite data. Make exact measurements and determine property boundaries. Provide data relevant to the shape, contour, gravitation, location, elevation, or dimension of land or land features on or near the earth's surface for engineering, mapmaking, mining, land evaluation, construction, and other purposes. Measure large areas of the Earth's surface using satellite observations, global navigation satellite systems (GNSS), light detection and ranging (LIDAR), or related sources. (Based on 9 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Analyze survey objectives and specifications to prepare survey proposals or to direct others in survey proposal preparation.
Direct or conduct surveys to establish legal boundaries for properties based on legal deeds and titles.
Survey bodies of water to determine navigable channels and to secure data for construction of breakwaters, piers, and other marine structures.
Locate and mark sites for geophysical prospecting activities, such as efforts to locate petroleum or other mineral products.
Conduct research in surveying and mapping methods, utilizing knowledge of photogrammetric map compilation and electronic data processing.
Develop criteria for survey methods, procedures, and the design and modification of survey instruments.
Compute geodetic measurements and interpret survey data to determine positions, shapes, and elevations of geomorphic and topographic features.
Train assistants and helpers, and direct their work in activities such as performing surveys or drafting maps.
Determine longitudes and latitudes of important features and boundaries in survey areas using theodolites, transits, levels, and satellite-based global positioning systems (GPS).
Establish fixed points for map-making, utilizing geodetic and engineering instruments.
Adjust surveying instruments to maintain accuracy.
Coordinate findings with engineering and architectural personnel, clients, and others concerned with projects.
Search legal records, survey records, and land titles to obtain information about property boundaries in areas to be surveyed.
Plan and conduct ground surveys to establish baselines, elevations, and other geodetic measurements.
Write descriptions of property boundary surveys for use in deeds, leases, or other legal documents.
Prepare or supervise the preparation of all data, charts, plots, maps, records, and documents related to surveys.
Direct aerial surveys of specified geographical areas and determine specifications for aerial photography equipment.
Review existing standards and controls for survey methods, recommending changes as needed.
Compute, retrace, or adjust existing surveys to ensure accuracy and continuity of data for engineering, surveying, or construction projects.
Conduct surveys to determine exact positions, measurements, elevations, and other features of land surfaces.
Determine orientation of land tracts, including position, boundaries, size, and shape using various surveying equipment.
Assess control data quality to determine the need for additional survey data for engineering and construction projects.
Analyze control or survey data to ensure adherence to project specifications.
Calculate exact horizontal and vertical positions of points on the Earth's surface.
Compute coordinates of control networks utilizing direct leveling or other geodetic survey techniques.
Maintain databases of geodetic information, including coordinate and descriptive data.
Provide training and interpretation in methods for observing and checking controls for geodetic coordinates.
Prepare progress or technical reports documenting survey activities and findings.
Request additional survey data when field errors occur or specifications are not maintained.
Verify the mathematical correctness of newly collected survey data.
Read literature and engage with peers to stay updated on technology, equipment, and systems.
Distribute compiled geodetic data to government agencies or the public.
Plan or direct the work of geodetic surveying staff, providing technical consultation as needed.
Calculate terrain characteristics such as heights, depths, and property lines.
Record survey results including the shape, contour, location, elevation, and dimensions of land features.
Inspect survey compositions to ensure accuracy and completeness.
Analyze aerial photographs for significant military, industrial, resource, or topographical data.
Compile data for map preparation from aerial photographs, survey notes, records, and reports.
Prepare and maintain sketches, maps, and legal descriptions to certify and assume liability for work performed.
Maintain daily logs and supplement inspection records with photographs detailing survey activities.
Review, interpret and compare plans, blueprints, and site layouts to ensure compliance with regulations.
Collect information needed for new surveys, utilizing previous data and maps.
Provide assistance in developing methods for conducting field surveys and inspecting sites.
Conduct inspections using various measuring devices to ensure adherence to legal and safety standards.
Inspect sites to verify compliance with project specifications and quality standards.
Analyze proposed site factors and design maps and graphs to illustrate findings.
Prepare job specification sheets and detailed reports related to surveys and construction activities.
Conduct interviews or communicate findings with relevant stakeholders regarding survey data.
Explain regulations to property owners and recommend remedial actions to address violations.
Calculate energy usage based on inspections of systems and report findings.
Inspect newly installed systems to ensure compliance with specifications, monitoring their performance post-installation.
Respond to queries from the public concerning surveys, boundaries, and assessments.

Prepare and maintain reports of investigation results, and records related to fire safety and prevention, while evaluating and inspecting properties to ensure compliance with fire codes, ordinances, regulations, and standards.

Conduct inspections and tests of fire protection and detection systems to verify compliance with appropriate laws, codes, and standards, and recommend changes to inspection and fire prevention procedures.
Evaluate the performance of firefighting personnel, supervise training, and communicate fire details to team members and dispatch centers.
Attend training classes and instruct fire department personnel in various aspects of firefighting, including emergency medical services, hazardous materials response, and relevant procedures.
Conduct investigations of suspected arson and fire hazards, test sites and materials to establish facts, and prepare related reports outlining findings.
Direct the scheduling, planning, and supervision of firefighting personnel, ensuring efficient work assignments and monitoring of expenditures.
Inspect and maintain firefighting equipment in good condition, conducting minor repairs as necessary.
Develop and coordinate fire prevention programs, including public education initiatives on fire safety and prevention measures.
Analyze fire conditions, participate in creating fire safety guidelines and evacuation schemes, and assess response requirements during emergencies.
Provide emergency medical services and participate directly in firefighting and rescue operations as part of an elite response team.

emerging_tasks.task: Determine orientation of tracts of land, including position, boundaries, size, and shape, using theodolites, electronic distance-measuring equipment, satellite-based positioning equipment, drones, land information systems, or other geodetic survey equipment.
Estimate resources, such as production hours, required for projects.
Use drone technology to capture high-resolution images and data for map creation and updating.
Evaluate the energy performance of buildings using modeling software.
Inspect structures to determine cause and origin of damage.
Operate drones for site surveying and inspection, providing detailed aerial views of project sites.
Use drone technology to capture aerial images or videos for creating detailed and accurate maps.
Deploy and monitor drones for aerial surveillance and assessment of fire situations.
